title: lastreopsis glabella
intro: Lastreopsis glabella, also known as the glabella fern, is a species of fern native to the tropical regions of Australia and New Guinea. It is a medicinal plant that has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, and its medicinal properties are attributed to the presence of bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Lastreopsis glabella include its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of fever, respiratory issues, and digestive problems. Herbal preparations of Lastreopsis glabella can be made by infusing the leaves in hot water to create a tea, or by drying and powdering the leaves to use as a supplement.
